title: Dataset of EEG recordings of pediatric patients with epilepsy based on the 10-20 system
description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings from 30 pediatric patients with focal or generalized epilepsy, with automated high-frequency oscillation (HFO) markings. The study investigates ripple rates (80-250 Hz) in scalp EEG as non-invasive biomarkers of epileptogenicity across the pediatric age range. The dataset includes 3 hours of sleep EEG data per subject with processed N3 sleep intervals and bipolar channel referencing, along with clinically validated HFO detections.
methods_description: EEG data were recorded during the first 3 hours of sleep in 30 children and adolescents with epilepsy. Raw data were recorded in .edf format using the 10-20 system. Processed data involved sleep stage identification, extraction of 5-minute intervals from N3 sleep stages, and bipolar referencing across 52 nearest-neighbor channels. High-frequency oscillations (ripples, 80-250 Hz) were detected using an automated, clinically validated HFO detector.
